tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    formulate() Function

    formulate.Rd

    This function formulates a character string (the boolean equation of a fault tree) into an R function that evaluates system failure. Handles AND and OR operations.

    formulate(formula)

    Arguments

    formula

    (Required) A character string containing the boolean logic equation of a fault tree. Should use * for AND operations, + for OR operations, and parentheses for grouping. The equation should contain only basic event names (no gate references). Typically output from equate().

    Value

    A function object that:

    • Accepts named arguments for each basic event in the formula

    • Each argument accepts numeric values (typically 0 or 1 for binary events)

    • Returns a numeric value representing the system state (typically 0 for no failure, >= 1 for failure)

    • Evaluates the boolean equation using R's arithmetic operators (* for AND, + for OR)

    The function can be called directly with event values or passed to calculate() to generate a complete truth table.

    Details

    This function converts a boolean equation string into an executable R function through the following process:

    • Extracts all unique event names from the formula by splitting on operators (+, *, (, ))

    • Creates formal arguments for the function, one for each unique event name

    • Sets the function body to parse and evaluate the formula string

    • Returns a function that can be called with named arguments for each event

    The function uses R's arithmetic operators where multiplication (*) represents AND logic and addition (+) represents OR logic. When called with binary inputs (0/1), the function returns 0 for no failure and a positive value for failure. The function preserves the order of operations through parentheses in the original formula.

    See also

    equate for generating the boolean equation string, calculate for generating truth tables from the function

    Examples

    
# Load dependencies
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyfault)
library(QCA)

# Load example data into our environment
data("fakenodes")
data("fakeedges")

# Build a boolean equation and convert it to an executable function
equation <- curate(nodes = fakenodes, edges = fakeedges) %>%
   equate()
formula <- formulate(equation)
formula
#> function (A, B, C, D) 
#> (((B * (C + D)) * (A + (B * C))))
#> <environment: 0x10d7c7438>

# Evaluate across all combinations of basic events
calculate(formula)
#> # A tibble: 16 × 5
#>        A     B     C     D outcome
#>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>
#>  1     0     1     1     0       1
#>  2     0     1     1     1       1
#>  3     1     1     0     1       1
#>  4     1     1     1     0       1
#>  5     1     1     1     1       1
#>  6     0     0     0     0       0
#>  7     0     0     0     1       0
#>  8     0     0     1     0       0
#>  9     0     0     1     1       0
#> 10     0     1     0     0       0
#> 11     0     1     0     1       0
#> 12     1     0     0     0       0
#> 13     1     0     0     1       0
#> 14     1     0     1     0       0
#> 15     1     0     1     1       0
#> 16     1     1     0     0       0
